label: Latissimus dorsi strain without rupture or neurovascular red flags
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated latissimus dorsi muscle strain.
No fracture/dislocation concern, deep infection, neurovascular compromise, high-energy trauma, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion_criteria: Fracture/dislocation concern, open wound, infection concern, neurovascular compromise, severe or rapidly worsening pain, high-energy trauma, immunocompromised host, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed orthopedic plan.
must_not_miss: Compartment syndrome or evolving emergency pain pattern.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care, sports medicine, orthopedics, therapy, urgent care, or the ED if symptoms are not improving or your clinician told you to recheck.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus supports musculoskeletal strain, bruise, or overuse framing, activity modification, clinician-directed pain control, and escalation for infection, severe pain, worsening function, or neurovascular symptoms. This expansion is limited to clinician-diagnosed uncomplicated musculoskeletal injury with fracture/dislocation, infection, neurovascular, pregnancy/pediatric, high-energy trauma, and specialist-directed pathways absent.

review.notes: Reviewed as a narrow production ontology phenotype. MedlinePlus supports musculoskeletal strain, bruise, or overuse framing, activity modification, clinician-directed pain control, and escalation for infection, severe pain, worsening function, or neurovascular symptoms. This expansion is limited to clinician-diagnosed uncomplicated musculoskeletal injury with fracture/dislocation, infection, neurovascular, pregnancy/pediatric, high-energy trauma, and specialist-directed pathways absent.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis of uncomplicated latissimus dorsi muscle strain
